text:
There once was a wise old owl named Oliver. He lived in a cozy tree in the heart of the forest. Every day, he would sit on his favorite branch and watch the world go by. Oliver had lots of knowledge to share.

One day, a young squirrel named Thistle asked him lots of questions. Oliver loved sharing wisdom, so he sat under his favorite branch every evening until the sun dipped below the trees.

Thistle listened intently. Oliver spoke of bravery, friendship, wisdom, and the beauty of being true to oneself. He also talked about the seasons, the stars, and the secrets trees hold.

One night, Thistle fell asleep in Oliver's shadow. Oliver smiled to himself and whispered, "The best things in life are those we find near."

And so Oliver settled down, ready for the night's rest. The forest was silent except for the soft rustling of leaves. And somewhere, deep in your belly, you may have felt a bit more cozy knowing that
